etymology.description: 英(英) (yīng, "hero; outstanding; English") is used here to refer to England or English, and by extension, the British currency. 鎊(镑) (bàng, "pound") refers specifically to the unit of currency known as the pound, marked by its symbol (£).
etymology.interpretation: 英(英) (yīng, "hero; outstanding; English") connects to England, thus linking to the British context, while 鎊(镑) (bàng, "pound") identifies the specific unit of British currency. Together, 英(英) (yīng, "English") and 鎊(镑) (bàng, "pound") form the term for the British currency, the pound sterling.

examples.chinese: 银行可以兑换英镑(銀行可以兌換英鎊)。
examples.english: The bank can exchange pound sterling.

examples.chinese: 英镑的汇率今天上涨了(英鎊的匯率今天上漲了)。
examples.pinyin: Yīngbàng de huìlǜ jīntiān shàngzhǎng le.
examples.english: The exchange rate of pound sterling rose today.

examples.chinese: 我用英镑买了一些纪念品(我用英鎊買了一些紀念品)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yòng yīngbàng mǎi le yīxiē jìniànpǐn.
examples.english: I bought some souvenirs with pound sterling.
